digest.component_or_area: operation-cert signing / attestation substrate (plan-review + completion-verifier verdicts)
propositions: Operation certificates are migrated from per-clone symmetric HMAC self-attestations to asymmetric Ed25519 signatures attributed to the signing environment and verifiable by any third party against that environment's public key.

A project may optionally require a certificate from a specific trusted environment for a gate, pinning trusted-environment public keys out-of-band in a Gerrit-gated .rebar/trusted_environments.yaml file (never the auto-pushed tickets branch), with verification against the pinned key rather than the certificate's self-claimed key_id.

A trusted rebar-mcp remote-execution path must fetch authoritative ticket state and code-at-commit itself and run the gate on that, so a developer cannot forge a passing verdict via client-supplied doctored inputs.

Certificates bind {ticket id, material fingerprint, merged-log position} to prevent replay onto another ticket or mutated state, and environment-key era-validity is evaluated at the SIGNATURE event's storage-anchor commit to close an anti-rollback gap.
The merge-gate CI job extends the shipped verify-identity gate to verify the required-environment certificate over the merged log and fail when a ticket that must carry one does not.

The legacy HMAC scheme is removed in a contract phase via an expand-contract migration so both operation-cert and authorship signatures are asymmetric, while the default local offline path stays a record and never wedges an operation.
